1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم سأل سائل بعذاب واقع
1.english: A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen
To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.
[It is] from Allah, owner of the ways of ascent.

1.arabic: تعرج الملائكة والروح إليه في يوم كان مقداره خمسين ألف سنة
1.english: The angels and the Spirit will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.
1.transliteration: Ta'rujul malaaa'ikatu war Roohu ilaihi fee yawmin kaana miqdaaruhoo khamseena alfa sanah

1.english: So be patient with gracious patience.
Indeed, they see it [as] distant,
But We see it [as] near.
On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,
And the mountains will be like wool,
And no friend will ask [anything of] a friend,

1.arabic: يبصرونهم ۚ يود المجرم لو يفتدي من عذاب يومئذ ببنيه
1.english: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children
1.transliteration: Yubassaroonahum; ya waddul mujrimu law yaftadee min 'azaabi yawma'izim bibaneeh

1.english: And his wife and his brother
And his nearest kindred who shelter him

1.arabic: ومن في الأرض جميعا ثم ينجيه
1.english: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.
1.transliteration: Wa man fil ardi jamee'an summa yunjeeh

1.english: No! Indeed, it is the Flame [of Hell],
A remover of exteriors.
It invites he who turned his back [on truth] and went away [from obedience]
And collected [wealth] and hoarded.
Indeed, mankind was created anxious:
When evil touches him, impatient,
And when good touches him, withholding [of it],
Except the observers of prayer -
Those who are constant in their prayer
And those within whose wealth is a known right
For the petitioner and the deprived -
And those who believe in the Day of Recompense

1.arabic: والذين هم من عذاب ربهم مشفقون
1.english: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -
Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not that from which one is safe -
And those who guard their private parts

1.arabic: إلا على أزواجهم أو ما ملكت أيمانهم فإنهم غير ملومين
1.english: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they are not to be blamed -
1.transliteration: Illaa 'alaaa azwaajihim aw maa malakat aymaanuhum fainnahum ghairu maloomeen

1.arabic: فمن ابتغى وراء ذلك فأولئك هم العادون
1.english: But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the transgressors -
1.transliteration: Famanib taghaa waraaa'a zaalika fa ulaaa'ika humul 'aadoon

1.english: And those who are to their trusts and promises attentive
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum li amaa naatihim wa 'ahdihim raa'oon

1.english: And those who are in their testimonies upright
And those who [carefully] maintain their prayer:
They will be in gardens, honored.
So what is [the matter] with those who disbelieve, hastening [from] before you, [O Muhammad],
1.transliteration: Famaa lil lazeena kafaroo qibalaka muhti'een

1.english: [To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?

1.arabic: أيطمع كل امرئ منهم أن يدخل جنة نعيم
1.english: Does every person among them aspire to enter a garden of pleasure?
1.transliteration: Ayatma'u kullum ri'im minhum anyyudkhala jannata Na'eem

1.english: No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.
1.transliteration: Kallaaa innaa khalaq nahum mimmaa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: فلا أقسم برب المشارق والمغارب إنا لقادرون
1.english: So I swear by the Lord of [all] risings and settings that indeed We are able
1.transliteration: Falaaa uqsimu bi Rabbil mashaariqi wal maghaaribi innaa laqaadiroon

1.arabic: على أن نبدل خيرا منهم وما نحن بمسبوقين
1.english: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.
1.transliteration: 'Alaaa an nubaddila khairam minhum wa maa Nahnu bimasbooqeen

1.arabic: فذرهم يخوضوا ويلعبوا حتى يلاقوا يومهم الذي يوعدون
1.english: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -
1.transliteration: Fazarhum yakhoodoo wa yal'aboo hattaa yulaaqoo yaw mahumul lazee yoo'adoon

1.arabic: يوم يخرجون من الأجداث سراعا كأنهم إلى نصب يوفضون
1.english: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.
1.transliteration: Yawma yakhrujoona minal ajdaasi siraa'an ka anna hum ilaa nusubiny yoofidoon

1.arabic: خاشعة أبصارهم ترهقهم ذلة ۚ ذلك اليوم الذي كانوا يوعدون
1.english: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.
1.transliteration: Khaashi'atan absaaruhum tarhaquhum zillah; zaalikal yawmul lazee kaanoo yoo'adoon
